title: Post rollout auto-rollback events to Slack (concise notification + links)
preview: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, ...
text: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, (2) the rollout SLO dashboard, and (3) the audit event details. Motivation: during the 2026-02 simulations and recent customer escalations, oncall often learned about auto-mitigations only after secondary alerts or customer reports; this closes the loop and reduces time-to-context while keeping noise bounded. Event source: consumes the new structured audit event emitted to the event stream (redwood PR-28435) with event_type=rollout.auto_action.*. The handler maps event subtypes to Slack severity and routing without paging by default (notify-only) to avoid paging storms. Mes...

title: Add billing meter regression scenarios for streaming, retries, and fallback routing
preview: Motivation: We’ve had multiple meter-related regressions where request-level behaviors (stream disconnects, server-side retry/fallback, and client retry with idempotency) caused double-counting or inconsistent attribution between (a) usage events and (b) the billing ledger. Unit/integration tests in the monorepo cov...
text:
Motivation: We’ve had multiple meter-related regressions where request-level behaviors (stream disconnects, server-side retry/fallback, and client retry with idempotency) caused double-counting or inconsistent attribution between (a) usage events and (b) the billing ledger. Unit/integration tests in the monorepo cover code paths, but we were missing an end-to-end canary that validates metering invariants against a live stack (gateway -> inference -> usage event emitter -> aggregation/export) under realistic failure modes.

This PR adds new perf-canary scenarios that intentionally trigger streaming + retry + fallback behaviors and then validate the expected metering invariants by querying the usage API / internal usage-events view within a bounded time window.

What’s included (new scenarios): 1) streaming_disconnect_no_double_count: Start a streaming chat completion, consume first N c...

title: Add metering drift Grafana dashboard + alert rules (drift, freshness, duplicates)
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing a metering data-quality view so Finance/RevOps + Eng can quickly answer: (a) are usage events in sync with the billing ledger, (b) is data arriving on time for close/invoice previews, and (c) are we seeing duplicate usage events consistent with idempotency regressions/backfills.

text:
Context
We’re standardizing a metering data-quality view so Finance/RevOps + Eng can quickly answer: (a) are usage events in sync with the billing ledger, (b) is data arriving on time for close/invoice previews, and (c) are we seeing duplicate usage events consistent with idempotency regressions/backfills.

This PR adds a Grafana dashboard (and initial alerting rules) for metering drift, freshness, and duplicate rates. It is intended to pair with the in-product Console sanity dashboard work (ENG-4415) and the scheduled drift job in the redwood monorepo (ENG-4421).

What’s included
- Dashboard: "Metering Sanity: Drift + Freshness" (folder: Billing/Metering)
 - Drift panels (usage_events vs billing_ledger aggregates)
 - Drift % by day (global)
 - Top-N accounts by absolute drift ($ and tokens) over last 24h / 7d
 - Drift by dimension: region, model, endpoint (chat/completions/embeddings...

title: Tiered SLO dashboards + deploy/incident annotations (Hosted vs Dedicated views)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a tiered SLO dashboard set to the observability-pack, including (1) Hosted SLO overview, (2) Dedicated SLO overview (tier-wide; no customer-level by default), and (3) a Dedicated vs Hosted comparison view. It also adds Grafana annotations for deploys and incidents so we can correlate latency sp...
text: Summary: This PR adds a tiered SLO dashboard set to the observability-pack, including (1) Hosted SLO overview, (2) Dedicated SLO overview (tier-wide; no customer-level by default), and (3) a Dedicated vs Hosted comparison view. It also adds Grafana annotations for deploys and incidents so we can correlate latency spikes / streaming disconnects / 5xx bursts with changes and incident timelines. Motivation: Part of the Q1 reliability milestone to formalize tiered SLOs + error budgets, we need canonical, tier-aware views that match the new SLI tagging at ingress and avoid metric cardinality blowups. This PR is intended to be the “default” internal dashboard surface linked from burn-rate alerts and runbooks. Out of scope: customer-facing Dedicated per-account dashboards and access control (tracked in ENG-4946 follow-ups) and any changes to SLI definitions/recording rules (those land in the...

title: Add Suggestions API + funnel Grafana dashboards and beta alerting
preview: Summary: Adds a first-pass set of Grafana dashboards + alert rules for the Optimization Suggestions beta, covering (1) Suggestions API health (latency/errors/payload) and (2) the Suggestions view funnel (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click), including breakdowns by org/model/route/region with conservative ca...
text: Summary: Adds a first-pass set of Grafana dashboards + alert rules for the Optimization Suggestions beta, covering (1) Suggestions API health (latency/errors/payload) and (2) the Suggestions view funnel (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click), including breakdowns by org/model/route/region with conservative cardinality controls. Motivation: during dogfood we had a p95 regression + early customer reports of “empty despite traffic” and “estimates look off”; we need a single place for SRE/Platform + product to validate the pipeline (compute freshness), the API (error/latency), and the UX funnel (are people seeing/using suggestions). Scope notes: This PR only adds dashboards/alerts in observability-pack; it assumes the backend/UI already emits the metrics/events (ENG-19402) and the Suggestions API exists (ENG-19341). No changes to application code here. Dashboards added: (A) Optimiz...

title: Audit log pipeline dashboards + alerts (ingestion latency, drop rate) with runbook links
preview:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR a...
text:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR adds:
- A Grafana dashboard for the audit-log ingestion pipeline (end-to-end delivery latency, ingest backlog, drop rate, retry/429/5xx, DLQ growth).
- Alert rules for delivery latency and drop rate with sane defaults + per-environment routing.
- Runbook links embedded in alert annotations and dashboard panel descriptions.

Key signals / definitions:
- delivery_latency_seconds: time from event emission at producer to ack at ingestion sink (measured via audit-ingest gateway metrics + exporter timestamps).
- drop_rate: fraction of events not successfully delivered after retry ...

title: Quality gates: dashboards + alert rules (pass rate, failure spikes, latency, flake rate)
preview: Adds first-class observability for Optimize quality gate runs. This PR introduces (1) a Grafana dashboard for the full gate-run funnel (created → running → passed/failed/overridden), (2) Prometheus alert rules for gate failure spikes, flake rate regressions, and gate execution latency/SLO breaches, and (3) runbook l...
text: Adds first-class observability for Optimize quality gate runs. This PR introduces (1) a Grafana dashboard for the full gate-run funnel (created → running → passed/failed/overridden), (2) Prometheus alert rules for gate failure spikes, flake rate regressions, and gate execution latency/SLO breaches, and (3) runbook links + consistent labels so we can slice by config_type (batching/cache/quant_profile), model_family, route_id, account_tier, and region. Motivation: we’re rolling out mandatory gating ahead of applying Optimize recommendations; we need to catch (a) real quality regressions, (b) eval-harness instability/flaky suites causing false blocks, and (c) infrastructure constraints causing timeouts (notably EU-West). Design notes: alerts avoid paging on single-run failures; they trigger on burn-rate / sustained increases and include a ‘possible flake’ annotation when rerun aggregatio...

title: Wire telemetry events for Suggestions funnel + server-side API usage logging
preview: Context: For the Optimize Suggestions beta, we need a closed-loop funnel to understand which recommendations are useful and where users drop off (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click). This PR wires client-side analytics events from the Console Suggestions list + details drawer, and adds server-side logging/m...
text: Context: For the Optimize Suggestions beta, we need a closed-loop funnel to understand which recommendations are useful and where users drop off (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click). This PR wires client-side analytics events from the Console Suggestions list + details drawer, and adds server-side logging/metrics on the /optimize/suggestions API for usage + debugging (without collecting request/response bodies). Scope: (1) UI events: suggestions_impression, suggestion_expand, suggestion_dismiss, suggestion_apply_click; (2) API logs + counters: optimize_suggestions.list requests, result counts, latency buckets, authz denied; (3) shared schema helpers to keep event props consistent across UI and backend. Non-goals: no PII, no raw prompts/responses, no customer-level savings numbers in analytics events (those remain in product surfaces only). Event properties (UI): org_id (hashe...

title: Post error budget burn summaries to #eng-sre on burn-rate alerts (dashboards + runbook links)
preview: Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget program, we’re standardizing burn-rate alerting and enforcement. Today the burn-rate alerts page the on-call, but triage is still slow because the initial signal doesn’t include (a) how fast we’re burning, (b) what’s driving it (latency vs errors vs streaming disconn...
text:
Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget program, we’re standardizing burn-rate alerting and enforcement. Today the burn-rate alerts page the on-call, but triage is still slow because the initial signal doesn’t include (a) how fast we’re burning, (b) what’s driving it (latency vs errors vs streaming disconnects), or (c) where to go next (dashboards/runbooks). This PR updates incident-bot to listen for burn-rate alert notifications and post a structured “burn summary” message into #eng-sre (and thread replies for updates), including deep links to the tiered SLO dashboards and the active mitigation runbook.

What this does: 1) Adds a new alert type handler for error budget burn-rate alerts (Prometheus Alertmanager payloads via our existing webhook). 2) Builds a canonical Slack message format: header with SLO name + tier + region, a short diagnosis (burn rate + window + budget r...

title: Add SDK retry/timeout/streaming disconnect dashboard panels + rollout alerts
preview: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity rollout (standard retries/backoff, timeout model, and streaming disconnect handling), SRE requested first-class dashboards and conservative alerts to monitor load impact and customer-facing failure rates during beta/stable releases. This PR adds (1) new panels to the...
text:
Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity rollout (standard retries/backoff, timeout model, and streaming disconnect handling), SRE requested first-class dashboards and conservative alerts to monitor load impact and customer-facing failure rates during beta/stable releases. This PR adds (1) new panels to the existing "SDK Client Health" Grafana dashboard for retry rate, timeout rate, and streaming disconnect reasons, (2) companion alert rules with rollout-friendly thresholds and burn-rate style paging, and (3) a small set of recording rules to make the queries cheaper/consistent across environments.

What’s included: - Dashboard panels: Retry rate (by sdk_language, endpoint, model, status_code), Retry attempts distribution (p50/p95 of retry_count per request), Timeout rate (by sdk_language, timeout_scope=overall|connect|read), Timeout duration (histogram heatmap where availabl...

title: perf-canary: add paging (Sev2/Sev1) alert rules for sustained per-model regressions
preview: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Mot...
text: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Motivation: During phase-1 rollout we saw warning-level regressions that were actionable when they sustained (real tail-latency impact), but the existing rules were designed to be noisy-tolerant and non-paging. This PR implements the paging path that SRE approved in the paging policy thread and is required for rollout gates integration. Alerting model: We page only for top-model + sustained regressions where we have enough canary volume to reduce false positives, and we gate on a canary-health signal (telemetry freshness + request loop healthy) to avoid paging on broken canary...

title: Add Usage Insights freshness/quality dashboard + alerts (lag + reconciliation drift)
preview: Motivation: Usage Insights is going into beta with interactive console queries backed by hourly/day partitions and reconciliation checks against billing. We need a standard dashboard + alert bundle in observability-pack so SRE/on-call can quickly answer: (1) is data fresh, (2) are we dropping/late-ingesting events b...
text:
Motivation: Usage Insights is going into beta with interactive console queries backed by hourly/day partitions and reconciliation checks against billing. We need a standard dashboard + alert bundle in observability-pack so SRE/on-call can quickly answer: (1) is data fresh, (2) are we dropping/late-ingesting events by route/model, (3) are we drifting vs billing totals beyond tolerance, and (4) are query endpoints timing out for common ranges.

This PR adds a Grafana dashboard and alert rule templates for freshness lag, late event rate, reconciliation drift, and query p95 latency. The dashboard is designed to be imported into customer-facing/internal stacks with minimal per-cluster edits (templated variables for env/region/deployment_mode/workspace_id).

What’s included: 1) Dashboard: “Usage Insights — Data Freshness & Quality” (Grafana JSON) Panels: Aggregation lag (p50/p95) by dataset...

title: Add Shadow Traffic quality delta dashboards + scoring lag panels (cardinality-safe)
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw r...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw route_id, customer_id).

This is intended to support beta oncall readiness and help triage the two main failure classes seen during dogfood/beta:
1) quality deltas missing/stale due to scoring lag or ingestion lag
2) metrics backend pressure due to accidental high-cardinality dimensions

## What’s included
- New "Shadow Traffic / Quality Deltas" dashboard:
 - Δ quality score (shadow - control) over time with confidence bands (when emitted)
 - refusal rate delta, format adherence delta
 - sample size panels (n_control, n_shadow) + effective sample rate
 - breakdowns limited t...

title: Policy engine: emit v1 audit events for routing policy + fallback updates (diff + cardinality checks)
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing audit logging coverage for sensitive actions across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Routing policy changes (incl. fallback rules) are security-relevant because they can alter what models serve which requests, potentially impacting data handling boundaries and reliability controls.

This PR inst...
text:
Context
We’re standardizing audit logging coverage for sensitive actions across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Routing policy changes (incl. fallback rules) are security-relevant because they can alter what models serve which requests, potentially impacting data handling boundaries and reliability controls.

This PR instruments the policy engine to emit schema v1 audit events for routing policy CRUD + publish operations, with:
- A bounded diff generator (before/after) to support investigations
- Cardinality guards to prevent high-cardinality payloads from polluting downstream SIEMs
- Consistent actor/target semantics + correlation IDs per the v1 standard

What’s included
- Audit events emitted for:
 - Routing policy create/update/delete
 - Fallback configuration update (primary/fallback model sets, failure-mode rules)
 - Allow/deny list changes for model routing
 - Policy publish/activate ...

title: perf-canary: add warning-level alert rules (dampening + min sample size) routed to #eng-runtime
preview:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sampl...
text:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sample conditions
- Alertmanager routing to Slack #eng-runtime
- Runbook + dashboard links in annotations

This is intended to be enabled first in us-east with a limited model list (per ENG-4880 rollout plan). Paging rules are tracked separately (pr-19183).

## Motivation / context
During early perf-canary rollout we saw false positives driven by low volume, baseline drift, and transient telemetry delays. The warning rules in this PR aim to be:
- Low-noise: only fire when there’s enough data
- Actionable: include baseline vs observed deltas + direct links
- Safe: warning-only ch...

title: Add baseline fetch + regression evaluator (min samples, rolling window, pct thresholds)
preview: Implements the baseline lookup path and the first pass of regression evaluation logic for perf-canary. This is the missing piece between “we can generate load + emit raw latency/tps metrics” and “we can decide whether something is actually regressing (and do so with low noise)”. The evaluator is intentionally conser...
text:
Implements the baseline lookup path and the first pass of regression evaluation logic for perf-canary. This is the missing piece between “we can generate load + emit raw latency/tps metrics” and “we can decide whether something is actually regressing (and do so with low noise)”. The evaluator is intentionally conservative for phase 1: it enforces minimum sample sizes, uses a rolling window over recent canary runs, and compares observed vs baseline using percent-change thresholds per metric.

Context / why: We’ve seen early canary runs spike due to batch composition variance and telemetry ingestion jitter. We need a consistent evaluation step that (a) won’t flap on small sample sizes, (b) is explicit about confidence, and (c) produces a stable verdict we can wire into alert rules + rollout gates.

What’s included:
- Baseline client: fetches baseline artifacts from the Baseline Store se...

title: Add Optimize suggestions API contract (schema + pagination/sorting)
preview: Context: Console is adding a new Suggestions view (beta) under Optimize. This PR introduces the backend API contract the Console will consume: endpoint shape, response schema for savings + risk, and server-side pagination/sorting. Scope is intentionally contract + plumbing only; RBAC enforcement and the compute job ...
text: Context: Console is adding a new Suggestions view (beta) under Optimize. This PR introduces the backend API contract the Console will consume: endpoint shape, response schema for savings + risk, and server-side pagination/sorting. Scope is intentionally contract + plumbing only; RBAC enforcement and the compute job that populates suggestions land in follow-ups (ENG-19377 / ENG-19455). API: GET /v1/optimize/suggestions Parameters: org_id (required), project_id (optional), environment (optional), route_id (optional), model (optional), suggestion_type (optional: batching|caching), risk_level (optional: low|medium|high), sort (optional), limit (default 25, max 100), cursor (opaque). Sorting: sort=estimated_savings_usd_per_day:desc (default), risk_level:asc, confidence:desc, stale_as_of:desc. Pagination: cursor-based (stable, opaque) backed by (sort_key, suggestion_id) to ensure determinis...

title: Add tier/deployment_mode tags to SLI metrics at ingress (and propagate through request context)
preview: Context: As part of the Tiered SLO + Error Budget program, we need to compute SLIs (success rate, latency, streaming reliability) separately for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity). Today most gateway metrics are emitted without a reliable tier signal, and routing/fallback can further obscure the origin...
text: Context: As part of the Tiered SLO + Error Budget program, we need to compute SLIs (success rate, latency, streaming reliability) separately for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity). Today most gateway metrics are emitted without a reliable tier signal, and routing/fallback can further obscure the original customer tier in downstream services. This PR adds tier/deployment mode tags to ingress SLI metrics and ensures the tags are attached to the request context so they can be propagated into routing, traces, and downstream metrics. Summary of changes: (1) Introduce a canonical tier classifier at ingress that maps requests into tier={hosted|dedicated} and deployment_mode={hosted_api|dedicated_pool} (naming aligned with ADR-0142 draft). (2) Add these tags to the gateway SLI metric set for request count, request duration histograms, and streaming termination counters. (3) Prop...

title: Audit logging: register sensitive-action event types and reject unknown event_type values
preview: Summary: Introduces a centralized registry for audit log event_type values (sensitive actions taxonomy) and enforces that all emitted audit events use a registered event_type. Enforcement happens in two places: (1) build-time lint/codegen to prevent new event types from being introduced ad-hoc, and (2) runtime valid...
text:
Summary: Introduces a centralized registry for audit log event_type values (sensitive actions taxonomy) and enforces that all emitted audit events use a registered event_type. Enforcement happens in two places: (1) build-time lint/codegen to prevent new event types from being introduced ad-hoc, and (2) runtime validation in the shared audit event emitter to optionally reject (or warn on) unknown types.

Motivation: As we drive to 100% coverage for sensitive actions (ENG-4822), we need consistent semantics across services and deployments (Hosted/Dedicated/Private). In the current state, event_type strings are free-form. This makes SIEM normalization brittle and makes coverage dashboards noisy (slight spelling variants create “new” types). Centralizing the taxonomy also reduces cardinality risk and improves backcompat guarantees.

Design: - Source-of-truth registry lives in repo at audi...

title: perf-canary: add QPS rate limits + per-region capacity budget (GPU overhead guardrail)
preview: Context: As we move perf-canary from staging into a production region, we need hard guardrails so the canary cannot consume meaningful GPU capacity or accidentally fan out due to config mistakes. This PR implements (1) explicit QPS caps with a token-bucket limiter, and (2) a per-region capacity budget that converts ...
text:
Context: As we move perf-canary from staging into a production region, we need hard guardrails so the canary cannot consume meaningful GPU capacity or accidentally fan out due to config mistakes. This PR implements (1) explicit QPS caps with a token-bucket limiter, and (2) a per-region capacity budget that converts an overhead target (e.g., 0.5% of region GPU capacity) into an allowed request rate/concurrency for the canary. The goal is: safe by default, predictable overhead, and an immediate kill-switch when budgets are exceeded.

What this adds (high-level): - Rate limiting: token bucket limiter at the request scheduler layer (covers both steady-state QPS and burst). - Capacity budget: `capacity_budget` config per region (defaults to 0.003 = 0.3% until we tune) with optional overrides per tier/model group. - Budget accounting: estimates GPU-seconds/request using (a) last known basel...

title: Add hot-route protection dashboards + alert rules (enterprise tier + shed reasons)
preview: Context: As part of Hot-route Capacity Protection (ENG-4926), SRE needs a consistent set of dashboards + alert rules for protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) that make it easy to answer: (1) are enterprise routes staying within SLO under fleet pressure, (2) are we shedding/throttling t...
text: Context: As part of Hot-route Capacity Protection (ENG-4926), SRE needs a consistent set of dashboards + alert rules for protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) that make it easy to answer: (1) are enterprise routes staying within SLO under fleet pressure, (2) are we shedding/throttling the right tier first, and (3) what is the reason breakdown (quota vs overload vs admission control) when 429s occur. This PR adds Grafana dashboard definitions (jsonnet) and Prometheus alert rules for the new metrics emitted by gateway/routing/runtime (see redwood PRs for instrumentation and 429 reason codes). Scope: dashboards (per-route/per-tier latency + error + shed), annotations for protection activation events, and paging rules focused on enterprise degradation (not expected best-effort shedding). What’s included: (A) Dashboards: 1) Hot Route Protection – Overview: req...

text:
Context: This is part of the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation + early retention). We need a standardized, self-serve dashboard that answers: (1) where do new signups drop in the funnel, (2) which experiment cohorts/variants moved the needle, and (3) how do we catch regressions quickly after email/nudge/retargeting launches.

The dashboard focuses on signup -> API key created -> first successful request -> first 100k tokens within 7d, plus TTFT distributions.

Dashboard updates: - New row: “Activation funnel (new signups)” with conversion panels per step (absolute counts + rates), and a time series of overall activation_rate_7d (first_request within 7d of signup). - Breakdown panels: conversion b...

title: perf-canary dashboards: add per-model p95 latency + tokens/sec panels (with breakdowns + runbook links)
preview:
## Summary
Adds the initial Grafana dashboard panels for the production performance canary. The goal is fast triage for per-model regressions (p95 latency and tokens/sec) with enough breakdowns to distinguish kernel/runtime execution regressions from scheduler/queueing and infra issues.

This PR is dashboards-only (...
text:
## Summary
Adds the initial Grafana dashboard panels for the production performance canary. The goal is fast triage for per-model regressions (p95 latency and tokens/sec) with enough breakdowns to distinguish kernel/runtime execution regressions from scheduler/queueing and infra issues.

This PR is dashboards-only (no alert rules). Alerts are tracked separately so we can iterate on panel queries/labels before we encode anything into paging logic.

## What’s included
- New dashboard: **Perf Canary / Per-model Performance (Prod)**
 - Topline panels per selected model:
 - End-to-end latency p50/p95/p99 (histogram quantiles)
 - Runtime-only latency p50/p95/p99 (when available)
 - Tokens/sec (overall) + tokens/sec (decode-only) where emitted
 - Breakdown panels:
 - Prefill latency vs decode latency (stacked / side-by-side)
 - Queueing time (scheduler) and queueing fraction of end-to-end
 -...

title: Control plane: config surface for perf-canary target models (by region/tier) + validation + audit logging
preview: Summary: This PR adds a first-class control-plane config surface for defining which models the perf-canary should exercise, scoped by region + capacity tier, with strict validation and audit logging. This unblocks phase-1 rollout in us-east with a small model set and gives SRE/runtime a safe way to expand coverage w...
text: Summary: This PR adds a first-class control-plane config surface for defining which models the perf-canary should exercise, scoped by region + capacity tier, with strict validation and audit logging. This unblocks phase-1 rollout in us-east with a small model set and gives SRE/runtime a safe way to expand coverage without code changes. Motivation: We need a single source of truth for the canary target list that (a) is auditable, (b) prevents accidental wide fanout (capacity burn / metric cardinality), and (c) allows staged rollout per region and tier. Previously, target lists were hardcoded in service config and required redeploys; that’s too risky for fast tuning. Design overview: Introduces a new config kind PerfCanaryTargets stored in the control-plane config service. Adds an admin write API (RBAC-gated) for setting/updating targets and a read API scoped to the perf-canary service ...

title: Generate perf-canary baselines from controlled prod-like load runs
preview: Summary: This PR adds a repeatable baseline generator job in benchmark-lab that computes per-model performance baseline distributions from a controlled prod-like load run. The output artifacts are written in a stable, versioned format that perf-canary can consume for regression evaluation (min samples + percent-chan...
text: Summary: This PR adds a repeatable baseline generator job in benchmark-lab that computes per-model performance baseline distributions from a controlled prod-like load run. The output artifacts are written in a stable, versioned format that perf-canary can consume for regression evaluation (min samples + percent-change thresholds). Motivation: perf-canary needs a durable baseline per (model, model_version, runtime_build, hardware_sku, region) that reflects real scheduler behavior (queueing/batching) without using customer traffic. Early experiments were using ad-hoc queries on ephemeral metrics, which made it hard to reproduce baselines and triggered baseline drift/cardinality issues during rollout. This job makes baselines an explicit artifact with an audit trail and a consistent schema. Scope / What’s included: (1) New job `baselinegen` that ingests a benchmark-lab load-run result bu...

title: Add region + hardware SKU dimension config (with fanout safeguards)
preview: ## Summary This PR adds first-class configuration for running perf-canary across multiple **regions** and **hardware SKUs**, while ensuring we don’t accidentally fan out to every region/SKU combination (and burn the overhead budget). The canary runner now supports: (1) Target selection by `region` and `hardware_sku`...
text:
## Summary This PR adds first-class configuration for running perf-canary across multiple **regions** and **hardware SKUs**, while ensuring we don’t accidentally fan out to every region/SKU combination (and burn the overhead budget). The canary runner now supports: (1) Target selection by `region` and `hardware_sku` (optional), (2) A hard fanout guard that defaults to conservative behavior, and (3) Safer defaults for Helm/Env-based deployments. This is needed for Phase 1 rollout where we want to start in `us-east` on a single SKU, then expand gradually to `eu-west` and additional SKUs once baselines/thresholds are tuned.

## Motivation / Context Today the canary targets are effectively “global” (implicitly whatever the control plane routes to). That makes it hard to: - Attribute regressions to fleet changes (SKU-specific) vs runtime changes - Set region-specific baselines (telemetry v...

title: Add log redaction + no-PII assertions for perf-canary request/telemetry path
preview: Motivation: perf-canary must never emit or persist customer data. While the canary uses synthetic prompts, we still had a few sharp edges where (a) debug logging could print request bodies on errors and (b) some error/reporting paths would include raw prompt text / user-provided identifiers if they ever slipped thro...
text: Motivation: perf-canary must never emit or persist customer data. While the canary uses synthetic prompts, we still had a few sharp edges where (a) debug logging could print request bodies on errors and (b) some error/reporting paths would include raw prompt text / user-provided identifiers if they ever slipped through config. This PR adds explicit redaction + hard assertions so we fail-closed and keep logs/metrics safe. Summary of changes: (1) Introduce a centralized redaction helper for log fields and error payloads; (2) Add runtime assertions that enforce "synthetic-only" prompt sources (no external prompt injection), with an opt-out only for local dev; (3) Ensure we never log request bodies, prompt strings, or headers that may contain identifiers; (4) Add "safe fields" allowlist for alert webhook payloads so incident-bot never receives prompt content; (5) Add tests that simulate e...

title: Add internal perf-canary auth scope + restricted service account for inference reads
preview: Context: The perf-canary service (see perf-canary repo PRs) needs to call internal inference endpoints continuously from prod. We do not want to reuse broad internal scopes (or customer-style API keys) because (a) blast radius is too large, (b) auditing is noisy, and (c) it complicates incident response when we need...
text:
Context: The perf-canary service (see perf-canary repo PRs) needs to call internal inference endpoints continuously from prod. We do not want to reuse broad internal scopes (or customer-style API keys) because (a) blast radius is too large, (b) auditing is noisy, and (c) it complicates incident response when we need to quickly disable only canary traffic. This PR introduces a restricted auth scope + service account intended solely for synthetic canary inference reads.

What’s in this PR:
- New auth scope: `inference.canary:read` (internal-only). This scope can only be minted for service accounts (not user tokens) and is denied in external auth flows.
- New service account type + default policy bundle: `svc-perf-canary` (control-plane managed). The policy allows:
 - POST `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/completions` against internal inference gateways
 - streaming responses
 - read-only ac...

title: perf-canary/telemetry: add per-model metrics aggregation + normalization helper
preview: Motivation: perf-canary and the Grafana dashboard queries need a consistent definition of per-model metrics across services (canary runner, runtime telemetry pipeline, and alert evaluation). We currently have multiple slightly-different implementations of: unit conversions (ms vs s), histogram bucket alignment, labe...
text: Motivation: perf-canary and the Grafana dashboard queries need a consistent definition of per-model metrics across services (canary runner, runtime telemetry pipeline, and alert evaluation). We currently have multiple slightly-different implementations of: unit conversions (ms vs s), histogram bucket alignment, label normalization (model vs model_id vs model_version), and derived signals (tokens/sec from token_count + duration). These differences are causing alert/query mismatches and make regression triage slower. This PR introduces a shared helper for per-model aggregation + normalization, intended to be used by perf-canary (PR-19218) and by the metrics pipeline/queries feeding dashboards/alerts. Summary of changes: (1) Add PerModelNormalizer + helpers to produce canonical metric names, units, and histogram buckets; (2) Add derived metric builders (e2e_latency_ms, runtime_latency_ms...

title: Add synthetic prompt generator + prompt-shape buckets (seedable workload profiles)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a synthetic prompt-shape generator and a seedable workload profile so perf-canary runs are reproducible across regions/hosts and can be tuned without touching code. The goal is to approximate production request shapes (prefill/decode mix, prompt length buckets) without using any customer prompt...
text: Summary: This PR adds a synthetic prompt-shape generator and a seedable workload profile so perf-canary runs are reproducible across regions/hosts and can be tuned without touching code. The goal is to approximate production request shapes (prefill/decode mix, prompt length buckets) without using any customer prompts or identifiers. Why: Baselines and regression detection require stable distributions. Early canary scaffolding used a fixed prompt and random lengths, which made p95 latency and tokens/sec variance too high and made comparisons across runtime versions noisy. What’s included: 1) PromptShape + WorkloadProfile config types (YAML) with explicit length buckets and weights; 2) Seeded RNG for selecting shapes, generation params, and per-run determinism; 3) A small library that produces “prompt skeletons” (shape only) that can be filled by a static, non-sensitive text corpus ship...
links: Next PR: pr-19212-emit-metrics-latency-tokens-per-sec.json (wires shapes into metric breakdowns)

title: Emit metrics for end-to-end latency and tokens/sec (per-model) with cardinality guardrails
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-pass metric emission for perf-canary runs: end-to-end latency histograms and tokens/sec (plus supporting counters) with per-model attribution. Metrics are emitted from the canary process (not the runtime) so we can alert even when runtime telemetry pipelines are delayed. Motivation: We ne...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-pass metric emission for perf-canary runs: end-to-end latency histograms and tokens/sec (plus supporting counters) with per-model attribution. Metrics are emitted from the canary process (not the runtime) so we can alert even when runtime telemetry pipelines are delayed. Motivation: We need low-noise, per-model signals for p50/p95/p99 latency and throughput (tokens/sec) before we can wire dashboards/alerting. This also sets the label policy up front to avoid a repeat of past cardinality blowups (e.g., per-prompt identifiers or unconstrained model strings). Metrics added (Prometheus/OpenTelemetry bridge): perf_canary_request_e2e_latency_ms (histogram; attributes: model_id, model_version, region, tier, hardware_sku, outcome); perf_canary_request_tokens_per_second (histogram; computed from generated_tokens / decode_time; same attributes); perf_canary_generated...

title: Runtime: emit build + kernel selection/version tags in telemetry
preview: Context: perf-canary + SRE triage needs to quickly answer “what changed?” when p95 latency or tokens/sec regresses. Today we can see model/version + hardware, but we can’t reliably correlate to runtime build or kernel selection changes (especially when selection is dynamic). This PR adds low-cardinality tags to runt...
text: Context: perf-canary + SRE triage needs to quickly answer “what changed?” when p95 latency or tokens/sec regresses. Today we can see model/version + hardware, but we can’t reliably correlate to runtime build or kernel selection changes (especially when selection is dynamic). This PR adds low-cardinality tags to runtime telemetry (metrics + traces + request debug logs) to identify the runtime build and the kernel selection outcome for a request. These tags are explicitly designed for per-model aggregation and alert attribution and should be stable across rollouts. What’s included: (1) Runtime build tags: runtime.build_id (semver-ish build label from build pipeline), runtime.git_sha (short SHA), runtime.build_flavor (release|debug), runtime.feature_set (compile-time feature bitset exported as a short string; see below). (2) Kernel selection identifiers: kernel.bundle_version (version st...

title: Add audit event schema library + JSON Schema validator (v1 draft)
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a shared audit event library in the monorepo to standardize emission of security audit logs across services. It adds (1) strongly-typed structs for AuditEvent v1 (draft) with consistent field naming, (2) a JSON Schema artifact + validator that runs at runtime (and in tests) to catch malfo...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a shared audit event library in the monorepo to standardize emission of security audit logs across services. It adds (1) strongly-typed structs for AuditEvent v1 (draft) with consistent field naming, (2) a JSON Schema artifact + validator that runs at runtime (and in tests) to catch malformed events early, and (3) example producers + unit tests to demonstrate correct usage in the control-plane and API gateway. Motivation: We’ve had drift across services (missing actor fields, inconsistent correlation IDs, ad-hoc target naming), which makes downstream normalization/export and customer SIEM mapping brittle. This is the foundation for ENG-4822+ (event_type registry + lints) and the service-by-service instrumentation rollouts. Scope: Adds new package `pkg/auditlog` (types, helpers, schema); adds `schemas/audit/audit-event-v1.schema.json`; adds test utilities an...

title: Bootstrap perf-canary service skeleton (config + request loop)
preview: Summary: This PR bootstraps the initial perf-canary service skeleton: repo layout, config parsing/validation, and a basic request loop that can hit internal inference endpoints on a schedule. This is intentionally minimal (no metrics emission yet, no baseline evaluation yet) so we can land the scaffolding early and ...
text:
Summary: This PR bootstraps the initial perf-canary service skeleton: repo layout, config parsing/validation, and a basic request loop that can hit internal inference endpoints on a schedule. This is intentionally minimal (no metrics emission yet, no baseline evaluation yet) so we can land the scaffolding early and iterate behind feature flags.

Context: We need a lightweight, always-on canary workload in prod to catch serving-runtime regressions (p95 latency + tokens/sec) before broad customer impact. This PR is the first building block: a small service that can execute synthetic requests against a curated model list and report per-run status via logs. Follow-ups will add: (1) synthetic prompt generation (shape-only; no customer prompts), (2) structured metrics + histograms, (3) baseline fetch + regression evaluation, and (4) rate limiting/capacity budgeting.

What’s in this PR:
- Ne...

title: runtime: export scheduler queueing-time metrics + batch-size distribution
preview: Motivation: perf-canary is showing end-to-end latency regressions that are hard to attribute (kernel/runtime execution vs scheduler pressure). This PR exports scheduler queueing-time metrics and batch-size distribution at the runtime layer so we can: (1) split latency into queueing vs execution for canary attributio...
text: Motivation: perf-canary is showing end-to-end latency regressions that are hard to attribute (kernel/runtime execution vs scheduler pressure). This PR exports scheduler queueing-time metrics and batch-size distribution at the runtime layer so we can: (1) split latency into queueing vs execution for canary attribution, (2) detect regressions caused by batching heuristics / admission control changes, (3) avoid relying on sampling-heavy trace-only approaches. What’s included: - New histogram metric for scheduler queueing time (enqueue -> execution start). - New histogram metric for batch size at execution start (effective batch after coalescing). - Structured dimension set aligned with telemetry cardinality constraints (no per-request identifiers). - Wiring to existing runtime metrics registry + export path. - Unit tests for metric emission + bounds (no negative durations, no zero/negati...

title: Add schema + SIEM mappings for offline bundle verification audit events
preview: Context: Redwood Private offline bundle signing/verifying is landing for air-gapped installs. The installer now emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success + specific failure reasons). Customers who forward audit logs to a SIEM via audit-log-exporter need these events to be recognized, normali...
text:
Context: Redwood Private offline bundle signing/verifying is landing for air-gapped installs. The installer now emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success + specific failure reasons). Customers who forward audit logs to a SIEM via audit-log-exporter need these events to be recognized, normalized, and exported in a stable schema.

What this PR does:
- Adds new event definitions to audit-log-exporter’s schema catalog for bundle verification outcomes.
- Adds normalization/mapping rules for JSON (native), CEF, and LEEF output modes.
- Ensures failure reasons are exported as a bounded enum + optional details field (redaction-aware).
- Updates unit tests + golden fixtures for all output formats.

New events (normalized):
- redwood.private.bundle_verification.succeeded
- redwood.private.bundle_verification.failed

Normalized fields:
- event.name (string)
- event.outco...

title: Post overload-protection activations to #incidents with dashboards + runbook links
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a new incident-bot event handler for Hot-route Capacity Protection activation events emitted by routing/gateway. When protection enters an active state (or exits), incident-bot posts a structured Slack message to `#incidents` with:
- route_group + route (when available), region, tier class, a...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a new incident-bot event handler for Hot-route Capacity Protection activation events emitted by routing/gateway. When protection enters an active state (or exits), incident-bot posts a structured Slack message to `#incidents` with:
- route_group + route (when available), region, tier class, and activation type
- primary reason code(s) (quota vs overload) and protection mode (admission control vs shedding)
- current/peak metrics snapshot (shed_rate, 429_rate, p95 latency, queue depth where present)
- links to the SRE dashboard + runbook section
- a dedupe key to avoid paging storms during flappy thresholds

This is intended to reduce time-to-diagnosis during overload events and make it obvious when best-effort is being throttled to preserve enterprise SLOs.

## Context
Hot-route capacity protection is rolling out behind flags (see ENG-4927). During the us-east c...

title: Add perf-canary alert handler (Slack threads, dedupe, runbook links)
preview:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit ...
text:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit alerts as a webhook payload (model + region + tier + metric + observed vs baseline + confidence + baseline version/runtime tags). Before this PR, incident-bot treated canary payloads as generic webhooks which led to noisy, non-threaded posts and no reliable dedupe key, making it hard to triage during regressions and especially during baseline drift/threshold tuning.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a new webhook route + handler for source=perf-canary (Content-Type: application/json). 2) Builds a stable dedupe key and thread key so repeat evaluations (same model/metric/region/sev...

title: Policy engine: add priority classes and per-route budgets (deterministic decision logging + validation)
preview: Summary: This PR extends the policy engine to support (a) explicit priority classes (enterprise/standard/best-effort/internal) and (b) per-route budgets that can be consumed deterministically during admission control / overload protection. It also adds deterministic decision logging (stable reason codes + structured...
text: Summary: This PR extends the policy engine to support (a) explicit priority classes (enterprise/standard/best-effort/internal) and (b) per-route budgets that can be consumed deterministically during admission control / overload protection. It also adds deterministic decision logging (stable reason codes + structured fields) and a schema validation layer to keep policies auditable for enterprise escalations. Motivation: Hot-route capacity protection requires a single, explainable place to answer: "given tenant, route group, and current capacity signals, should we admit / throttle / shed / fallback". We need to do this with minimal per-request overhead and without introducing high-cardinality telemetry. This PR focuses on correctness + determinism; performance tuning (memoization/batched lookups) will follow. What’s in scope: 1) Policy schema additions: - priority_class: enum {INTERNAL,...

title: Add status summary metadata to incident events
preview:
Context
We’re implementing auto-drafted, publishable post-incident summaries (ENG-4823). The incident-bot can already export a timeline of events (see PR #19540), but the events don’t currently carry the structured metadata we need to generate consistent “Impact / Timeline / Mitigation” blocks without hand-parsing f...
text:
Context
We’re implementing auto-drafted, publishable post-incident summaries (ENG-4823). The incident-bot can already export a timeline of events (see PR #19540), but the events don’t currently carry the structured metadata we need to generate consistent “Impact / Timeline / Mitigation” blocks without hand-parsing free-form notes.

This PR adds optional, structured “status summary” metadata to incident events emitted by the redwood control plane so incident-bot can draft post-incident summaries with fewer manual edits and lower risk of accidentally including sensitive detail.

What’s included
- Adds optional fields to incident event payloads:
 - impact_start (RFC3339 timestamp; when customer impact started, as determined by IC/SRE)
 - impact_end (RFC3339 timestamp; when customer impact ended / SLO recovered)
 - affected_routes (array of route identifiers, e.g. "chat.completions", "emb...

title: Anti-flap logic (windows + hysteresis) for status-component health signals
preview: Motivation: Our current component health rollups (used to recommend public status changes and to populate status-page dashboards) are too twitchy. Short-lived spikes (e.g., 2–3 minutes of elevated 5xx or a brief p95 regression) can flip a component between Operational ↔ Degraded multiple times, which creates noisy o...
text: Motivation: Our current component health rollups (used to recommend public status changes and to populate status-page dashboards) are too twitchy. Short-lived spikes (e.g., 2–3 minutes of elevated 5xx or a brief p95 regression) can flip a component between Operational ↔ Degraded multiple times, which creates noisy on-call workflows and undermines customer trust in the status page. This PR implements anti-flap windows and hysteresis in the status-signal evaluator so we only transition component state when a condition persists long enough, and we require a healthier condition to recover than the one that caused the degradation. Summary of changes: Adds a small state machine around each status signal (errors + latency) with configurable (1) trigger window (min duration / min samples) and (2) recovery window (min duration / min samples) plus (3) hysteresis thresholds (degrade_threshold vs...

title: Add region-aware streaming disconnect/error metrics and map to status components
preview: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we need streaming-specific signals that can be cleanly attributed to region and rolled up to public components (Hosted API > Streaming, per region). Today we have aggregate gateway error counts and some websocket-level counters, but they are not consistently...
text: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we need streaming-specific signals that can be cleanly attributed to region and rolled up to public components (Hosted API > Streaming, per region). Today we have aggregate gateway error counts and some websocket-level counters, but they are not consistently labeled by region and do not distinguish client disconnects vs server-side stream aborts/timeouts, which has led to slow/incorrect status updates (see SUP-28549 and the 2025-11-07 US-East streaming degradation follow-ups). This PR adds region-aware counters for streaming failures/disconnects, standardizes label keys (region, capability=streaming, component_id), and documents how these metrics roll up into the status component health views used by on-call and incident-bot drafts. Scope: 1) Emit new metrics from the streaming path (SSE + websocket) with consistent region lab...

title: Emit status component tags on API gateway metrics
preview: Motivation: Status page transparency upgrades require us to attribute customer-visible symptoms (errors/latency/stream disconnects) to public components consistently. Today most of our gateway metrics are only tagged by route + model + raw service, which makes it hard to (a) drive component-level dashboards/alerts a...
text: Motivation: Status page transparency upgrades require us to attribute customer-visible symptoms (errors/latency/stream disconnects) to public components consistently. Today most of our gateway metrics are only tagged by route + model + raw service, which makes it hard to (a) drive component-level dashboards/alerts and (b) generate incident summaries that match the status page taxonomy. This PR adds low-cardinality tags to gateway metrics so the observability-pack and incident tooling can compute component health without guesswork. Scope: (1) Add tags to gateway metrics: component_id, capability, region. (2) Derive these tags from existing request context + the service-to-component mapping (no new external calls on the hot path). (3) Add guardrails to avoid cardinality explosions (no raw path tags; capability is an enum; component_id is from registry; region is from gateway config). (4...

title: Add overload shedding dashboards + initial alert rules (breaker opens, shed rate)
preview: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding reliability program, we need first-class visibility into (a) when we are shedding traffic vs failing implicitly, and (b) when breakers are opening/half-opening/closing across regions/models/tiers. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and initial Prometheus a...
text: Context: As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding reliability program, we need first-class visibility into (a) when we are shedding traffic vs failing implicitly, and (b) when breakers are opening/half-opening/closing across regions/models/tiers. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and initial Prometheus alert rules for overload protection signals. Scope: (1) Dashboards: Add "Hosted API • Overload Protection" dashboard group with panels for shed rate, breaker open rate, admission decisions, runtime saturation proxies (queue depth / token saturation / memory pressure proxy), and top shed reasons. Includes recommended drilldowns by region and route family, and a safe set of labels (bounded cardinality). (2) Alerts: Add first-pass paging + ticket alerts for (i) sustained breaker-open rate, (ii) elevated shed rate for paid tiers, (iii) Retry-After header missing on overload resp...

title: Include KMS rotation audit events in export schema + parsers
preview: Context: Redwood is adding explicit audit log events for KMS rotation and cutover toggles (see redwood PR #18541). Several regulated customers ingest Redwood audit logs into their SIEM (Splunk/Elastic/Sentinel) via this exporter. Without schema updates, these events get emitted as `unknown_event_type` and are either...
text:
Context: Redwood is adding explicit audit log events for KMS rotation and cutover toggles (see redwood PR #18541). Several regulated customers ingest Redwood audit logs into their SIEM (Splunk/Elastic/Sentinel) via this exporter. Without schema updates, these events get emitted as `unknown_event_type` and are either dropped or lose critical fields needed for rotation evidence.

This PR updates the export schema and parsing/mapping code so KMS rotation events are exported as first-class, typed records with stable fields suitable for correlation and evidence collection.

What changed (high level):
- Added new event types to the exporter schema:
 - `kms.key_version.updated`
 - `kms.rotation.cutover.enabled`
 - `kms.rotation.cutover.disabled`
 - `kms.rotation.dual_read.enabled`
 - `kms.rotation.dual_read.disabled`
- Extended the parser to recognize these events from Redwood audit log enve...

title: Announce circuit breaker state transitions to #incidents and #eng-oncall
preview:
Context
As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding program (ENG-4821), we need a low-latency, human-visible signal when breakers start shedding (OPEN) and when they recover (HALF_OPEN/CLOSED). The current workflow relies on dashboards/alerts only; during incidents this increases time-to-diagnosis and con...
text:
Context
As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding program (ENG-4821), we need a low-latency, human-visible signal when breakers start shedding (OPEN) and when they recover (HALF_OPEN/CLOSED). The current workflow relies on dashboards/alerts only; during incidents this increases time-to-diagnosis and contributes to duplicate triage.

This PR teaches incident-bot to announce breaker state transitions into Slack (#incidents and #eng-oncall) with:
- Transition summary (CLOSED → OPEN, OPEN → HALF_OPEN, HALF_OPEN → CLOSED)
- Scope (region, route, model selector, tenant/tier when available)
- Top contributing signal(s) (queue depth, token/sec saturation, dependency health)
- Links to dashboards and the oncall runbook
- A short “recommended actions” block aligned with the runbook

Behavior
- Posts OPEN transitions to both #incidents and #eng-oncall.
- Posts recovery transitions (...

title: runtime: export scheduler queue depth + saturation metrics (bounded cardinality)
preview: Context: As part of hosted overload protection work, SRE + routing need a small set of reliable, low-cardinality runtime saturation signals that correlate with tail latency / admission failures. Today we have a few high-cardinality per-worker metrics (and gaps for queue depth) that are difficult to alert on and have...
text:
Context: As part of hosted overload protection work, SRE + routing need a small set of reliable, low-cardinality runtime saturation signals that correlate with tail latency / admission failures. Today we have a few high-cardinality per-worker metrics (and gaps for queue depth) that are difficult to alert on and have caused Prom churn during bursts.

This PR adds scheduler queue depth + saturation gauges to the serving runtime and introduces an aggregation layer to bound metric cardinality. These are intended to be the primary signals consumed by the gateway/policy engine and dashboards (shed rate vs saturation), and to support dark-launch tuning.

What’s in this PR:
- New runtime metrics emitted from the scheduler:
 - runtime_sched_prefill_queue_depth (gauge)
 - runtime_sched_decode_queue_depth (gauge)
 - runtime_sched_inflight_requests (gauge)
 - runtime_sched_token_throughput_utiliz...

title: Add KMS latency/error dashboards + alert rules (per env/region)
preview: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext...
text: Context: As part of the KMS integration hardening initiative we needed first-class dependency visibility for KMS across Hosted/Dedicated/Private paths. This PR adds a KMS Dependency Health dashboard and alert rules that align to the canonical error taxonomy (AccessDenied/NotFound/Throttling/Timeout/InvalidCiphertext/Unknown) and our metric namespacing conventions. Scope: (1) Dashboard: Overview + per env/region panels for request volume, latency (p50/p95/p99), error rate by category, throttling signals, and circuit breaker state; (2) Alerts: page-worthy vs ticket-worthy thresholds, with environment-aware routing (prod pages, staging tickets) and burn-rate style windows where appropriate; (3) Operator ergonomics: runbook links + log/trace pivot hints (trace_id, kms_key_id, provider, region, service); (4) Documentation: short README explaining how to use/tune the alerts and what to chec...

title: Dashboards: correlate model version change rate with rollout phases and incident/regression signals
preview: Context: As part of the model metadata schema + changelog generator rollout, we now emit structured model change events (promotions, rollbacks, metadata edits) and rollout phase transitions. Support and oncall repeatedly asked for a fast way to answer: “did this latency/regression coincide with a model version promo...
text: Context: As part of the model metadata schema + changelog generator rollout, we now emit structured model change events (promotions, rollbacks, metadata edits) and rollout phase transitions. Support and oncall repeatedly asked for a fast way to answer: “did this latency/regression coincide with a model version promotion or rollout phase change?” This PR adds Grafana panels + recording rules to correlate: (1) model version change rate, (2) rollout phase transitions (canary/A-B/GA/rollback), and (3) incident/regression signals (5xx, timeouts, p95/p99 latency, eval-guardrail failures when present). What’s included: - New dashboard: “Model Rollout Correlation” with panels for change rate over time, rollout phase annotations, and ‘before/after’ latency + error overlays. - Update existing “LLM Serving Overview” dashboard with a compact panel showing model version change rate by deployment_m...

title: Audit log: emit model change events + internal admin query endpoints
preview: Problem: Support/Security need authoritative, queryable audit history for model metadata changes (create/update/promote/deprecate/rollback) to answer enterprise escalations (e.g., “what changed, who did it, when, and in which deployment?”) and to satisfy audit/retention expectations for Private/VPC. Today we emit so...
text:
Problem: Support/Security need authoritative, queryable audit history for model metadata changes (create/update/promote/deprecate/rollback) to answer enterprise escalations (e.g., “what changed, who did it, when, and in which deployment?”) and to satisfy audit/retention expectations for Private/VPC. Today we emit some control-plane events for changelog generation, but they’re not shaped as audit events, not consistently retained, and there’s no internal support surface to query by model/version/workspace.

This PR adds (1) first-class audit log events for model metadata and promotion lifecycle, and (2) an internal admin-only query API used by Support/Compliance to retrieve the audit timeline. Customer-visible release notes remain generated elsewhere; this is the compliance/support trace.

Scope:
- Emit audit events on: model_version.created, model_version.metadata_updated, model_versi...
